bullocks-oriole.size: Small songbird, slightly larger than a sparrow
bullocks-oriole.habitat: Open woodlands, riparian groves, and parklands of western North America
bullocks-oriole.migration: Migratory; breeds in western North America and winters in Mexico and Central America

yellow-headed-blackbird.habitat: Freshwater marshes and wetlands with dense emergent vegetation
yellow-headed-blackbird.diet: Seeds and insects; forages on the ground in fields and marshes
yellow-headed-blackbird.migration: Migratory; breeds in western North America and winters in the southwestern US and Mexico

cactus-wren.size: Largest wren in the United States
cactus-wren.habitat: Desert scrub of the southwestern US and Mexico, especially among saguaro and cholla cactus
cactus-wren.diet: Primarily insects, supplemented by some plant matter; meets water needs from its food
cactus-wren.migration: Non-migratory; resident year-round in established territories

verdin.size: Very small songbird, smaller than a sparrow
verdin.habitat: Desert scrub and thorny brush of the American Southwest and Mexico
verdin.diet: Insects, spiders, and small berries gleaned from shrubs

phainopepla.size: Slender medium-small songbird, larger than a sparrow
phainopepla.habitat: Desert scrub with mistletoe-laden trees, and oak woodlands in California
phainopepla.diet: Primarily mistletoe berries; also catches insects in aerial sallies
phainopepla.migration: Partially migratory; some populations move between desert and woodland habitats seasonally

vermilion-flycatcher.habitat: Riparian areas, semi-open environments, and scrublands near water in southern North America and South America
vermilion-flycatcher.diet: Insectivorous; catches prey entirely in flight (aerial insectivore)
vermilion-flycatcher.migration: Mostly resident; northern populations may move short distances southward in winter

common-chaffinch.habitat: Woodlands, gardens, parks, and open farmland across Europe and the Palearctic
common-chaffinch.diet: Seeds outside breeding season; invertebrates, especially caterpillars, during breeding
common-chaffinch.migration: Partial migrant; birds in warmer regions are sedentary, northern breeders winter further south

european-greenfinch.habitat: Woodland edges, hedgerows, parks, and gardens across Europe, North Africa and Southwest Asia
european-greenfinch.diet: Seeds and berries; particularly fond of sunflower seeds at garden feeders
european-greenfinch.migration: Mainly resident; northern populations migrate south in winter

eurasian-bullfinch.habitat: Woodland, scrub, orchards, and gardens across Europe and the Palearctic
eurasian-bullfinch.diet: Seeds, berries, and buds, especially fruit tree buds in spring
eurasian-bullfinch.migration: Largely resident; some northern birds move south in winter

common-linnet.habitat: Open country with scrub, heathland, farmland, and hedgerows across Europe and Asia
common-linnet.diet: Seeds, especially flax and hemp; named for its fondness for these plants
common-linnet.migration: Partial migrant; northern and eastern populations move south and west in winter

eurasian-siskin.habitat: Forested areas, both coniferous and mixed woodland, across Europe and Eurosiberia
eurasian-siskin.diet: Seeds of all kinds, especially alder and conifers; an acrobatic feeder that hangs upside down
eurasian-siskin.migration: Partially migratory; migrates south in large numbers in some years, driven by food availability

hawfinch.size: 16.5–18 cm long; a large, bulky finch with a massive bill
hawfinch.habitat: Deciduous or mixed woodland, including parkland, with large trees, especially hornbeam
hawfinch.diet: Seeds and fruit kernels, especially cherry stones, cracked with its powerful bill
hawfinch.migration: Mainly resident in Europe; many Asian birds migrate further south in winter

yellowhammer.habitat: Open areas with shrubs or trees, farmland, hedgerows, and woodland edges across Eurasia
yellowhammer.diet: Mainly seeds, supplemented by invertebrates during the breeding season
yellowhammer.migration: Mostly resident in western Europe; eastern populations partially migrate south in winter

common-reed-bunting.habitat: Reedbeds and wetland margins; also drier open areas such as moorland and arable fields
common-reed-bunting.diet: Seeds and invertebrates; shifts to insects during the breeding season
common-reed-bunting.migration: Partial migrant; most birds move south in winter, but southern and western birds are resident

eurasian-tree-sparrow.size: Small passerine bird, slightly smaller than a house sparrow
eurasian-tree-sparrow.habitat: Lightly wooded open countryside and farmland in Europe; towns and cities in eastern Asia
eurasian-tree-sparrow.diet: Mainly seeds; also invertebrates, particularly during the breeding season
eurasian-tree-sparrow.migration: Largely resident; some populations make short local movements in winter

coal-tit.size: Very small passerine bird, one of the smallest tits
coal-tit.habitat: Forests, particularly coniferous woodland, throughout the temperate to subtropical Palearctic and North Africa
coal-tit.diet: Insects and spiders; also seeds and caches food for winter
coal-tit.migration: Largely resident; non-migratory throughout most of its range

long-tailed-tit.size: Small bird with a distinctively long tail; body very small, similar to a wren
long-tailed-tit.habitat: Deciduous and mixed woodlands, scrub, heathland, farmland, parks, and gardens across Eurasia
long-tailed-tit.diet: Insectivorous; feeds on insects, spiders, and other small invertebrates
long-tailed-tit.migration: Resident; largely non-migratory, forming roving family flocks in winter

dunnock.habitat: Hedgerows, gardens, woodland edges, and scrub throughout temperate Europe and into Asian Russia
dunnock.diet: Insects, worms, and small seeds gleaned from the ground
dunnock.migration: Largely resident across most of its range; some northern birds may move short distances

song-thrush.size: Medium-small thrush with brown upperparts and spotted underparts
song-thrush.habitat: Forests, gardens, and parks across the West Palearctic
song-thrush.diet: Omnivorous; earthworms, snails (broken on an anvil stone), insects, and berries
song-thrush.migration: Partially migratory; many birds winter in southern Europe, North Africa, and the Middle East

mistle-thrush.size: Large thrush; the largest thrush in Europe
mistle-thrush.habitat: Open woods, parks, hedges, and cultivated land across Europe, temperate Asia, and North Africa
mistle-thrush.diet: Wide variety of invertebrates, seeds, and berries, especially mistletoe, holly, rowan, and yew
mistle-thrush.migration: Mostly resident; northern and eastern populations migrate south in winter

redwing.size: Small thrush, slightly smaller than the song thrush
redwing.habitat: Woodland, farmland, and open ground; breeds in northern Eurasia, winters further south
redwing.diet: Earthworms, insects, and berries, especially in winter
redwing.migration: Strongly migratory; breeds in northern Europe and the Palearctic, winters in western and southern Europe

fieldfare.size: 25 cm (10 in) long; a large, robust thrush
fieldfare.habitat: Woodland and scrub for breeding in northern Europe and the Palearctic; open fields and farmland in winter
fieldfare.diet: Omnivorous; molluscs, insects, and earthworms in summer; berries, grain, and seeds in winter
fieldfare.migration: Strongly migratory; breeds in northern Europe, winters in western Europe, North Africa, and the Middle East

european-pied-flycatcher.habitat: Deciduous woodland, particularly oak woods, across Europe and the Western Palearctic
european-pied-flycatcher.diet: Mainly insectivorous; catches caterpillars, flies, spiders, ants, and bees
european-pied-flycatcher.migration: Migratory; breeds in Europe and winters mainly in tropical Africa

common-nightingale.size: Small passerine bird, slightly larger than a robin
common-nightingale.habitat: Dense thickets, woodland undergrowth, and scrub across Europe and into Asia
common-nightingale.diet: Insects, worms, and berries foraged on or near the ground
common-nightingale.migration: Migratory; breeds in Europe and western Asia, winters in sub-Saharan Africa

black-redstart.size: Small passerine bird, similar in size to a robin
black-redstart.habitat: Rocky habitats, cliffs, and built-up areas across Eurasia and North Africa
black-redstart.diet: Insects, spiders, and small berries; forages on the ground and from low perches
black-redstart.migration: Partial migrant; resident in milder parts, northern and eastern birds migrate south in winter

common-redstart.size: Small passerine bird, similar in size to a robin
common-redstart.habitat: Open woodland, parks, old orchards, and gardens across Europe and into Asia
common-redstart.diet: Insects, spiders, and small berries; forages on the ground and in trees
common-redstart.migration: Migratory; breeds in Europe and western Asia, winters in sub-Saharan Africa

european-stonechat.size: Small passerine bird, slightly smaller than a robin
european-stonechat.habitat: Heathland, gorse, scrub, and rough grassland across Europe and parts of North Africa
european-stonechat.diet: Insects, worms, and small berries; hunts from exposed perches
european-stonechat.migration: Partial migrant; some populations resident year-round, others move south in winter

northern-wheatear.habitat: Open stony country, moorland, tundra, and rocky slopes across Europe, Asia, and parts of North America
northern-wheatear.diet: Insectivorous; feeds on insects, spiders, and other small invertebrates on the ground
northern-wheatear.migration: Strongly migratory; all populations winter in Africa

common-house-martin.habitat: Open country and near human habitation across Europe, North Africa, and west-central Asia
common-house-martin.diet: Insectivorous; catches flying insects entirely in the air
common-house-martin.migration: Migratory; breeds in Europe and winters in sub-Saharan Africa and southwestern Asia

sand-martin.size: Small swallow-family bird, one of the smallest in its family
sand-martin.habitat: Open country near rivers, lakes, and sand banks across the Holarctic; nests in burrows in sandy cliffs
sand-martin.diet: Insectivorous; catches small flying insects in the air over water
sand-martin.migration: Migratory; breeds across the Holarctic and winters in eastern and southern Africa, southern Asia, and South America

common-swift.size: Medium-sized bird, somewhat larger than a barn swallow
common-swift.habitat: Aerial; breeds near buildings and cliffs in towns and villages across Europe and Asia
common-swift.diet: Insectivorous; catches all food in the air; feeds on flying insects and airborne spiders
common-swift.migration: Migratory; spends winter in sub-Saharan Africa; non-breeding birds may remain in continuous flight for up to ten months

white-wagtail.habitat: Open country, often near habitation and water; forages on bare ground and paved areas
white-wagtail.diet: Insectivorous; catches insects and other invertebrates on the ground
white-wagtail.migration: Partial migrant; resident in mildest parts of range, otherwise migrates to Africa

grey-wagtail.habitat: Always associated with running water when breeding; also lakes, coasts, and other watery habitats outside breeding season
grey-wagtail.diet: Insects and other invertebrates caught near water
grey-wagtail.migration: Partially migratory; several populations migrate to tropical Asia and Africa in winter

yellow-wagtail.size: 15–16 cm long; slender with a long, constantly wagging tail
yellow-wagtail.habitat: Open country near water, wet meadows, and damp grassland across temperate Europe and Asia
yellow-wagtail.diet: Insectivorous; catches insects on the ground and in low vegetation
yellow-wagtail.migration: Mostly migratory; winters in tropical Africa and southern Asia; small Egyptian population is resident

meadow-pipit.habitat: Open grassland, moorland, tundra, and upland areas across the Palearctic
meadow-pipit.diet: Insects and other invertebrates; some seeds, especially in winter
meadow-pipit.migration: Migratory over most of its range; winters in southern Europe, North Africa, and southwestern Asia; resident in western Europe

eurasian-skylark.size: Small passerine bird, streaked greyish-brown with a buff-white belly
eurasian-skylark.habitat: Open farmland, heathland, and grassland across Europe and the Palearctic
eurasian-skylark.diet: Seeds, leaves, and invertebrates; forages entirely on the ground
eurasian-skylark.migration: Partial migrant; many birds move south or to lower ground in winter

common-starling.size: About 20 cm (8 in) long; medium-sized passerine with glossy black, speckled plumage
common-starling.habitat: Open habitats, farmland, woodland, and urban areas across temperate Europe and the Palearctic
common-starling.migration: Resident in western and southern Europe; northeastern populations migrate south and west in winter

eurasian-magpie.size: Medium-large corvid; one of Europe's largest passerines
eurasian-magpie.habitat: Farmland, woodland edges, parks, and urban areas throughout northern Eurasia
eurasian-magpie.diet: Omnivorous; insects, worms, seeds, carrion, eggs, and nestlings

western-jackdaw.size: 34–39 cm (13–15 in) in length; a small crow
western-jackdaw.habitat: Farmland, open woodland, coastal cliffs, and urban settings across Europe, western Asia, and western North Africa
western-jackdaw.diet: Omnivorous; plant material, invertebrates, and food waste from urban areas
western-jackdaw.migration: Mostly resident; northern and eastern populations migrate south in winter

carrion-crow.size: Medium-large corvid, larger and stockier than the American crow
carrion-crow.habitat: Wide variety of habitats including farmland, woodland, coasts, and urban areas across western Europe and eastern Palearctic
carrion-crow.diet: Omnivorous; carrion, invertebrates, eggs, seeds, and refuse
carrion-crow.migration: Largely resident; non-migratory across most of its range

common-wood-pigeon.size: Large pigeon, the largest pigeon in Europe
common-wood-pigeon.habitat: Farmland, woodland, parks, and gardens across the western Palearctic
common-wood-pigeon.diet: Mainly plant material, including cereals, leaves, seeds, and berries
common-wood-pigeon.migration: Largely resident in western Europe; some northern birds move south in winter

eurasian-collared-dove.size: Medium-sized dove, larger than a turtle dove
eurasian-collared-dove.habitat: Urban and suburban areas, farmland, and gardens across Europe, Asia, and northern Africa
eurasian-collared-dove.diet: Mainly seeds and grain; also berries and some invertebrates
eurasian-collared-dove.migration: Largely resident; non-migratory following its remarkable 20th-century range expansion

european-turtle-dove.size: Small dove, the smallest European dove
european-turtle-dove.habitat: Woodland edges, scrub, hedgerows, and farmland across the southwestern Palearctic and North Africa
european-turtle-dove.diet: Primarily seeds; forages on the ground in fields and woodland clearings
european-turtle-dove.migration: Migratory; breeds in Europe and North Africa, winters in northern sub-Saharan Africa

common-cuckoo.size: Medium-sized bird, similar in size to a collared dove
common-cuckoo.habitat: Wide variety of open habitats including woodland, moorland, marshes, and farmland across Europe and Asia
common-cuckoo.diet: Insects, especially hairy caterpillars that most birds avoid
common-cuckoo.migration: Migratory; breeds in Europe and Asia, winters in Africa

great-spotted-woodpecker.size: Medium-sized woodpecker; pied black and white with a red belly patch
great-spotted-woodpecker.habitat: All types of woodland across the Palearctic including parts of North Africa
great-spotted-woodpecker.diet: Seeds (especially pine cone kernels), insect larvae excavated from wood, and occasionally eggs and chicks of other birds
great-spotted-woodpecker.migration: Mostly resident; some northern birds irrupt southward if conifer cone crop fails

european-green-woodpecker.size: Large woodpecker with vivid green plumage and a bright red crown
european-green-woodpecker.habitat: Open woodland, parkland, orchards, and grassland with nearby trees across Europe and western Palearctic
european-green-woodpecker.diet: Primarily ants and their larvae dug from the ground with its long tongue; rarely drums on trees

eurasian-wryneck.size: About 16–18 cm (6.3–7.1 in) in length; a small, cryptically-patterned woodpecker relative
eurasian-wryneck.habitat: Open countryside, woodland, and orchards across temperate Europe and Asia; also in northwestern Africa
eurasian-wryneck.diet: Primarily ants and other insects found in decaying wood or on the ground
eurasian-wryneck.migration: Mostly migratory; winters in tropical Africa and southern Asia; some populations resident in northwestern Africa

european-bee-eater.habitat: Open country with bare sandy banks for nesting, and open woodland across southern Europe, North Africa, and western Asia
european-bee-eater.diet: Bees, wasps, and other large flying insects; removes stings before swallowing prey
european-bee-eater.migration: Strongly migratory; breeds in southern Europe and Asia, winters in tropical Africa

european-roller.size: Medium-sized, stocky bird with brilliant blue and chestnut plumage
european-roller.habitat: Open country with trees, woodland edges, and dry savanna; avoids treeless plains
european-roller.diet: Large insects, small reptiles, frogs, and rodents caught on the ground
european-roller.migration: Migratory; breeds across Europe, the Maghreb, and central Asia, winters in southern Africa

eurasian-hoopoe.size: Distinctive medium-sized bird with a tall erectile crest and a long narrow downcurved bill
eurasian-hoopoe.habitat: Open countryside, orchards, olive groves, and parkland across Europe, Asia, and Africa
eurasian-hoopoe.diet: Insects, larvae, and small reptiles probed from the ground with its long bill
eurasian-hoopoe.migration: Migratory in the northern part of its range; resident in Africa and southern parts of its range

common-pheasant.size: Large gamebird; males are much larger than females with a long tail
common-pheasant.habitat: Woodland edges, farmland, and scrub; native to Asia, widely introduced as a gamebird worldwide
common-pheasant.diet: Seeds, berries, insects, and small vertebrates; forages on the ground

grey-partridge.habitat: Open farmland, grassland, and hedgerows across Europe and the Palearctic
grey-partridge.diet: Seeds, leaves, and invertebrates; insects are especially important for chicks

red-legged-partridge.size: Medium-sized, rotund gamebird with distinctive red legs and bill
red-legged-partridge.habitat: Open farmland, scrub, and stony hillsides across southwestern Europe and northern Africa
red-legged-partridge.diet: Seeds and plant material; young birds especially take insects for protein

common-quail.size: Small ground-nesting gamebird; the smallest European galliform
common-quail.habitat: Open farmland, grassland, and cereal fields across Europe and North Africa
common-quail.diet: Seeds, leaves, and invertebrates foraged from the ground
common-quail.migration: Mainly migratory; breeds in the western Palearctic and winters in Africa and southern India

western-capercaillie.size: Largest of all extant grouse species; males nearly twice the size of females
western-capercaillie.habitat: Dense coniferous and mixed forest across Europe and the Palearctic
western-capercaillie.diet: Pine needles, buds, berries, seeds, and invertebrates; diet shifts seasonally
western-capercaillie.migration: Largely resident; a ground-dwelling forest species that does not migrate

eurasian-woodcock.size: Medium-small wading bird with cryptic reddish-brown plumage
eurasian-woodcock.habitat: Damp woodland with thick leaf litter across temperate and subarctic Eurasia
eurasian-woodcock.diet: Earthworms and invertebrates probed from soft ground with its long sensitive bill
eurasian-woodcock.migration: Partial migrant; northern populations move south and west to milder areas in winter

northern-lapwing.size: Medium-sized wading bird with distinctive crest and broad rounded wings
northern-lapwing.habitat: Open farmland, wet meadows, moorland, and estuaries across temperate Eurosiberia
northern-lapwing.diet: Invertebrates, especially earthworms and insects, foraged from the ground
northern-lapwing.migration: Partial migrant; northern and eastern birds move south and west in winter

european-golden-plover.size: Relatively large plover; larger than American and Pacific golden plovers
european-golden-plover.habitat: Upland moorland and tundra for breeding; lowland farmland and grassland in winter
european-golden-plover.diet: Invertebrates, especially earthworms and insects; also some berries and seeds
european-golden-plover.migration: Migratory; breeds on northern moorland and tundra, winters on lower farmland and coasts further south

common-redshank.size: Medium-sized wading bird with distinctive red legs and bill base
common-redshank.habitat: Wet meadows, saltmarshes, estuaries, and mudflats across Eurasia
common-redshank.diet: Invertebrates including worms, crustaceans, and insects probed from mud and shallow water
common-redshank.migration: Partial migrant; northern populations move to coasts and south in winter

black-tailed-godwit.habitat: Breeds in fens, lake edges, damp meadows, moorlands, and bogs; winters on estuaries, swamps, and flooded areas
black-tailed-godwit.diet: Invertebrates, worms, and aquatic prey probed from mud with its long bill
black-tailed-godwit.migration: Strongly migratory; breeds from Iceland across Europe and central Asia, winters across the Indian subcontinent, Australia, and West Africa
